title: Slow-cooker pulled pork
subtitle: Pork shoulder with a dry rub, eight to ten hours on low
excerpt: A whole pork shoulder rubbed with smoked paprika, cumin, brown sugar, and garlic powder, slow-cooked for eight to ten hours until the fat has rendered and the meat shreds along the grain with two forks. Serve in buns with coleslaw or over rice.
sourceNotes: Pulled pork is a Southern American barbecue tradition that became a British home cooking and street food staple in the 2010s. The slow cooker version produces a result that would otherwise require a smoker or a full oven braise. Pork shoulder (Boston butt) is the correct cut — high fat content and collagen content makes it ideal for the long, low cook. The dry rub is applied the night before for the best flavour.

recipe.freezeNotes: Pull, cool, and freeze in portions. Reheat with a splash of the cooking juices.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dry rub can be applied the night before. Refrigerate uncovered for the best bark.

content.text: Place the pork fat-side up in the slow cooker. The fat renders down through the meat as it cooks, basting it from the inside. Fat-side down produces a drier result. No liquid is required — the pork generates all the moisture it needs over nine hours.

Mix the {{smoked-paprika}}, {{cumin-ground}}, {{garlic-powder}}, {{onion-powder}}, {{light-brown-sugar}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} together. Rub all over the {{pork-shoulder}}, pressing into the flesh. Place fat-side up in the slow cooker. Pour the {{cider-vinegar}} into the base of the pot — not over the meat. Cook on low for 8 to 10 hours, until the pork is completely tender and yields when pressed. Transfer to a board and rest for 15 minutes. Pull apart along the grain using two forks, discarding any large fat pieces. Return the pulled meat to the pot and toss with the cooking juices.

items.symptom: Pork still firm and not pulling at 8 hours
items.cause: Shoulder was very cold going in, or the joint is large
items.fix: Continue cooking for another 1 to 2 hours. Pulled pork cannot be overcooked — extra time only improves the texture

items.symptom: No bark or crust on the outside
items.cause: Slow cooker produces steam, not dry heat
items.fix: Spread the pulled meat on a foil-lined tray and place under a hot grill for 5 to 8 minutes to caramelise the surface. Watch closely

items.symptom: Meat dry despite the long cook
items.cause: Very lean shoulder, or fat-side placed down
items.fix: Always cook fat-side up. Toss the pulled meat with the cooking juices before serving to remoisten it

content.text: Pulled pork feeds a crowd from one piece of cheap meat. Start it before breakfast and it is ready for a late lunch or dinner. Serve in toasted buns with coleslaw and a small bowl of the cooking juices for dipping. Leftovers keep in the fridge for four days and reheat well with a splash of water to loosen.
